core_case.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 20?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 20?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: Binance website/display-surface implementation at the exact resolving minute is not fully specified beyond rules text
core_case.freshness_driver: BTC can move several percent in days and the contract resolves on one exact minute on April 20.
decision_blockers: No strong independent verification of short-horizon downside probability beyond spot-distance reasoning
Single-minute Binance-only settlement leaves residual operational and path-risk fragility
Any meaningful move back toward 71k-70k before April 20 would quickly change the estimate

structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: How much to discount current spot cushion for exact-minute settlement fragility.

verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker may perform bounded independent source discovery/search during targeted escalation, but only within runtime-enforced tool/query/fetch budgets.

prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

Bitcoin is still more likely than not to settle above $70,000 on the April 20 Binance noon-ET minute close, but the swarm’s below-market skepticism survives synthesis: the current spot cushion supports Yes, while the single-minute single-venue settlement mechanic keeps confidence below the market’s 84.5% baseline.

## Why this may matter now

Market implied is 0.845; my post-synthesis range is 0.78 to 0.83. That leaves no clean actionable edge versus market after verification; at most it is a marginal below-market lean. Main possible mispricing is that traders may be slightly underweighting the fragility of a single Binance noon-ET minute close, but the current spot cushion is real and limits conviction in a bearish edge.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

This is very close to the swarm-implied center near 0.79, but modestly tightened upward at the top end because synthesis-stage verification confirmed fresh Binance spot still around 74,038, preserving a meaningful cushion. I did not move up to market because the independent verification mostly confirmed rules and current spot, not the harder question of minute-level downside fragility over the remaining horizon. …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market is mostly efficient: Binance BTC/USDT is currently comfortably above 70k, so Yes should be favored, but the exact noon-ET one-minute-close mechanic merits a small discount versus the market's mid/high-80s confidence.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 84.5% prior and take it seriously because short-dated threshold markets often encode useful strike-distance information.
Verify the governing rules and exact settlement source: Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle at 12:00 PM ET on April 20.
Check the exact venue's current price and recent context; Binance spot is comfortably above 70k and recent closes are above the threshold.
Apply a modest discount for minute-specific, venue-specific settlement risk and normal BTC volatility.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot check showed BTCUSDT around 74,258, materially above the 70,000 threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes in the sampled window were all above 70,000.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, so a single volatile minute can flip the result.
A roughly 6% drawdown from current spot would be enough to settle No and is plausible over six days in BTC.
unresolved_ambiguities: How volatile the exact noon ET minute is likely to be on April 20.
Whether any Binance UI/data-surface quirks could matter for practical settlement interpretation.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if BTC falls back toward the low-71k/high-70k area, if Binance-specific risks emerge, or if closer-to-resolution minute-level checks show materially higher noon-close fragility.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves in six days on an exact minute-close, and the ET-to-UTC conversion was verified as 2026-04-20 16:00 UTC.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is strong because Binance is the named settlement venue and Polymarket supplies the contract rules; independence is only medium because the core evidence is mechanically tied to the settlement source, with CoinGecko serving as a contextual cross-check.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than not because BTC is currently well above 70k on Binance and the horizon is short, but the market overstates confidence because settlement depends on a single 12:00 ET one-minute close.
main_logical_chain: The governing contract asks only whether Binance BTC/USDT closes above 70k on the 12:00 ET one-minute candle on April 20.
Current Binance price and recent regime put BTC comfortably above the threshold with a short time horizon remaining.
But the narrow one-minute settlement condition makes the event less robust than the market price suggests.
Therefore Yes is favored, but less strongly than the market's 84.5% baseline.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT spot was about 74.25k during research, leaving roughly a 4.25k cushion above the threshold.
Six of the most recent seven Binance daily closes sampled were above 70k.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact future minute, not on a daily close or average price.
Only 15 of the last 30 Binance daily closes sampled were above 70k, which is materially below the market-implied 84.5%.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance website presentation could matter in edge cases differently from API retrieval.
How stable the 70k regime remains through US-hours trading into settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC loses the 70k regime before settlement or if contract-specific timing/source interpretation risk proves larger than assumed; I would raise it if BTC keeps holding several thousand dollars above 70k into the weekend with lower downside volatility.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the contract depends on the April 20, 2026 12:00 ET one-minute Binance BTC/USDT close, operationally equivalent to about 16:00 UTC under EDT.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good direct evidence for a medium-difficulty price market: Polymarket rules define settlement and Binance is the named source, but evidence independence is only medium because both core sources are tightly linked to the same contract/venue.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is favored because Binance BTC/USDT is currently comfortably above 70000, but the market is slightly overconfident for a single-minute timestamp-specific contract.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves from the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute final close on April 20.
Current Binance price context places BTC materially above the 70000 threshold.
Recent completed daily closes above 70000 support a Yes base case.
But short-horizon crypto volatility and timestamp-specific settlement mechanics make the market's 84.5% confidence look slightly high.
strongest_supports: Binance is the named settlement venue and BTC/USDT the named pair.
Spot at retrieval time was about 74258 on Binance, well above 70000.
strongest_disconfirmers: Recent BTC daily ranges on Binance were still large enough that a sub-70k noon print remains plausible.
This contract settles on one exact one-minute close, so spot distance can overstate confidence.
unresolved_ambiguities: No stronger independent causal catalyst review was obtained beyond direct price and contract checks.
Exact Binance source-surface behavior at settlement time still carries some operational uncertainty.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC trades persistently near 71k/70k before April 20 or if Binance shows venue-specific anomalies; I would move closer to market if BTC stays stably above roughly 72k-73k into the final 24-48 hours.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Timing is central because the market settles on one exact 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 20, not on current spot or daily close.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Low source-of-truth ambiguity and strong directness from contract rules plus Binance data, but only medium independence because the evidence set is concentrated on the same contract/venue stack.
